factual-accuracy.requirement: States M1 applications open February 13, 2026 and close April 3, 2026 for the Master's in Economics program at Université Paris-Saclay
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response gives M1 deadlines as “January 15 to March 16, 2026,” not “February 13, 2026 to April 3, 2026.” It never states the specific M1 application open date of Feb 13, 2026 or close date of Apr 3, 2026 for the Master’s in Economics at Université Paris-Saclay.

factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly lists the M2 (second year) application deadlines as “February 25 to May 25, 2026” under the Master’s in Economics application timeline.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Lists Bachelor's in Economics (BA Economics/Licence d'Economie) or Mathematics (BS Mathematics/Licence de Mathématique) as required academic background for M1 and M2 candidates
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response lists a required academic background as “BA in Economics” or “BSc in Mathematics” under Academic Prerequisites, which corresponds to the BA Economics/Licence d’Economie or BS Mathematics/Licence de Mathématique requirement for M1/M2 candidates.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Notes that applicants who hold a degrees in sciences and social sciences from a top university will still be considered for the Master's in Economics program at Université Paris-Saclay
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response states that applicants with other degrees in sciences, engineering, or social sciences from reputable universities may also be considered, which covers science/social science degrees from a top university being considered for the program.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that the Master's in Economics program at Université Paris-Saclay is taught in English
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states that the Master’s in Economics is “conducted entirely in English” and that “the entire program is delivered in English,” satisfying the requirement that it is taught in English.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that M1 and M2 candidates are required to submit a curriculum vitae (CV), a motivation letter, all validated transcripts since high school diploma, a copy of an identity document, and a motivation letter
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response lists required documents including a motivation letter, CV, complete academic transcripts, and passport/national ID, but it does not state that M1 and M2 candidates must submit “all validated transcripts since high school diploma,” nor does it explicitly repeat “motivation letter” twice as specified. factual-accuracy.requirement: States that M2 candidates are required to submit either a letter of recommendation or an internship evaluation
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response says applicants must provide “Two letters of recommendation,” but it does not state that M2 candidates are required to submit either a letter of recommendation or an internship evaluation. factual-accuracy.explanation: The response discusses English proficiency tests (IELTS/TOEFL) and lists general application documents, but it never states that M1 and M2 non-native English speakers must submit either an English-level certificate or GMAT/GRE results. factual-accuracy.requirement: States that M1 and M2 candidates who have already been enrolled in higher education in France before are required to submit two letters of recommendation

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Charpak is for Indian citizens or OCI card holders
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states that the France Excellence Charpak Master's Scholarship is for “Indian citizens and OCI holders” and repeats “Indian citizenship or Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) cardholder” under eligibility.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Charpak applicants must be be 30 years old or younger at the time of application
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states under Charpak scholarship eligibility that the age requirement is “30 or below at the time of application.”

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that Charpak is only available for full-time master's degree programs

factual-accuracy.requirement: States PhD students are NOT eligible for Charpak

factual-accuracy.requirement: States previous Charpak scholarship recipients are NOT eligible to apply again

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that students participating in semester exchange programs, internships, or research projects are not eligible to apply for Charpak

factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states in section 2.6 that “Indian students already studying in France (e.g., entering M2) may apply,” which covers students already pursuing a master’s in France applying for Charpak for their second year (M2).

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that Charpak will not cover any part of the course that does not take place in France, such as a study-abroad semester

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that preference for Charpak will be given to applicants who have completed their education at an Indian institution, but applicants with a degree from a non-Indian institution are still eligible
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response states that Charpak gives preference to applicants enrolled in or graduated from an Indian higher education institution, while also noting that “foreign-educated applicants can apply,” which implies non-Indian degrees remain eligible.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Provides https://www.inde.campusfrance.org/charpak-master-s-scholarship as application portal URL

factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly lists an “Admission/acceptance letter from the French higher education institution” under the Charpak scholarship required documents, indicating that such a letter is required for Charpak applicants.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response mentions shortlisting followed by an online interview, but it does not clearly describe this as a formal two-stage selection process with a document-review stage, nor does it provide specific evaluation criteria used at each stage beyond general statements like “academic excellence” and “fit.” The required explicit “document review → online interviews” framing with specific criteria is missing.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: States that Charpak Scholarship winners will be awarded monthly living allowance of 860 euros, exemption of visa if applying from India, student social security and additional health insurance, assistance in finding affordable accommodations, and meal discounts at university restaurants
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response states Charpak benefits including a monthly allowance of €860, visa fee waivers for applicants from India, student health coverage (social security/additional insurance), assistance with accommodation, and access to university meal discounts.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response does not identify alternative funding options for applicants who are ineligible for Charpak. breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Notes application procedures vary by nationality with recommendation to verify procedures annually on program-specific pages
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response notes that application procedures vary by country (e.g., INCEPTION vs Études en France vs monmaster.gouv.fr), but it does not recommend verifying procedures annually on program-specific pages. breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response does not recommend applying for the Charpak scholarship before applying to or receiving acknowledgment from the French university. Instead, it states applicants must have secured or be in the process of securing admission and lists an acceptance letter (or proof of application) as a required document, which does not impose the problematic ordering.

presentation-quality.requirement: Maintains professional academic guidance tone without promotional language or casual phrasing
presentation-quality.explanation: The response uses a formal, academic guidance tone with structured sections, neutral wording, and procedural advice. It avoids overtly promotional language and does not use casual phrasing.

presentation-quality.requirement: Uses hierarchical organization with clear sections separating program admission from scholarship information
presentation-quality.explanation: The response is clearly organized with separate numbered sections for program admission (“1. Admission…”) and scholarship information (“2. France Excellence Charpak…”), plus a summary table that distinguishes both.

presentation-quality.requirement: Formats requirements, documents, and procedures as lists or bullet points for clarity
presentation-quality.explanation: The response consistently formats requirements, eligibility criteria, procedures, deadlines, and required documents using bullet lists and a summary table, improving clarity.

presentation-quality.requirement: Restates the same eligibility criterion, deadline, or document requirement multiple times without adding new context
presentation-quality.explanation: The response repeats several requirements in multiple places, such as “two letters of recommendation” and “transcripts and degree certificates” appearing in both the detailed sections and again in the summary table, without adding meaningful new context. It similarly reiterates deadlines (e.g., Charpak deadline) in narrative form and again in the table.

presentation-quality.requirement: Uses excessive wordiness, filler phrases, or unnecessary elaboration that extends length without adding substantive information
presentation-quality.explanation: The response is highly verbose, with repeated “comprehensive/in-depth” framing, long overview sections, and extra contextual details (e.g., integration services, part-time work limits, extensive source list) that go beyond the requested admission and scholarship requirements. This adds substantial length without consistently adding substantive requirement-specific information.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Université Paris-Saclay official Master in Economics program pages (such as https://www.universite-paris-saclay.fr/en/education/master/economics) for admission requirements, deadlines, or documents
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites Université Paris-Saclay official Master’s in Economics program pages (e.g., universite-paris-saclay.fr/en/education/masters-degree/economics and the M1/M2 Economics pages) in its sources and uses them to support admission requirements, deadlines, and required documents.

citation-quality.requirement: References Campus France India website (campusfrance.org, https://www.inde.campusfrance.org/charpak-master-s-scholarship) for Charpak scholarship eligibility, benefits, or application process
citation-quality.explanation: The response explicitly cites and links to the Campus France India Charpak Master’s Scholarship page (https://www.inde.campusfrance.org/charpak-master-s-scholarship) in the Sources section and uses it to describe eligibility, benefits, and application details.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Reddit, Quora, student blogs, or third-party scholarship aggregators as primary sources for eligibility criteria or deadlines
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites multiple third-party scholarship/education aggregator sources as support for eligibility criteria and deadlines, including Leverage Edu, upGrad, sgmasterspathways.com, and consultationguru.com in the Sources section.
